title: hieracium tomentosum subsp. pteropogon
intro: Hieracium tomentosum subsp. pteropogon, also known as the woolly hawkweed or downy hawkweed, is a subspecies of perennial plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes. The health benefits of Hieracium tomentosum subsp. pteropogon include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antiviral activity. Herbal preparations of this plant can be found in the form of teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health issues, including skin conditions and respiratory problems.
